_description: Pre-computed model lookup tables for dashboard integration without Python runtime
_updated: Weather-grounded, drop-date-aware DM model with seasonal phase amplification. All calibration uses actual weather from Open-Meteo (4 priority markets averaged). Incorporates 2026 actuals through 3/26 and actual weather through 3/29. Peak DM multiplier recalibrated from 1.65 to 1.35 based on 7 days of wave 2 actuals. Week 13 organic baseline raised from 139 to 150 based on 4 days of actuals.

weather_multipliers._note: Lead multiplier vs typical weekday baseline. Apply to seasonal baseline for organic. For DM, dampen by dm_weather_sensitivity.

dm_weather_sensitivity._note: DM response is less weather-sensitive than organic because it is trigger-based (mail in hand). Effective DM weather multiplier = 1 + (organic_mult - 1) * sensitivity. Calibrated from 2026 wave 1: 3/12 snow day had 63 DM leads (organic would predict near-zero at 0.41x). At sensitivity=0.3, dm_mult=0.823 which produces a weather-neutral estimate of 76.5 — consistent with the response curve shape.

dm_phase_multipliers._note: DM response rate varies by season phase. Calibrated: wave 1 (Ramp) produced 608 DM from 400K pieces, weather-neutral ~602. Wave 2 (Peak) 7-day actual implied multiplier = 1.35x (days 0-4 averaged 1.87x front-loaded, days 5-6 dropped to 0.86x). Prior estimate of 1.65x was based on first 3 days only and over-predicted the back half of the response window.

dow_multipliers._note: Day-of-week multiplier vs weekday average. Updated with 2026 actuals. Saturday and Sunday higher than original model due to DM weekend response (people open mail on weekends) and digital channel growth (Facebook, Next Door active 7 days).

seasonal_baseline_weekly._note: ORGANIC-ONLY weekday baseline (excludes DM). Week 13 validated at 153 (median of 4 days implied baselines: 154, 193, 152, 144 -- Tuesday outlier excluded from median). Week 14 set at 140 (between 5Y avg of 127 and week 13 validated value; no 2026 data yet). All other weeks are 5Y avg with +10% growth. Apply DOW, weather, and warm streak multipliers on top.

dm_addon_weekly._note: LEGACY fallback: Average DM leads per weekday when mail is in home (5Y historical average + 10% growth baked in). Used when no specific wave data is available for a date. Superseded by dm_waves_2026 + dm_phase_multipliers for dates with known drop schedules.

dm_drops_2026._note: Individual DM drops for 2026 season. Each drop has a drop_date, in_home_date (+3 days), and pieces. The forecast engine computes DM contribution per day using weather_neutral_base_per_100k * (pieces/100K) * phase_mult * dm_weather_mult * response_curve_weight. Drops can be added/modified via the dashboard UI.

response_curve_pct._note: Day-by-day % of total drop DM response (days 0-13 from in-home date). Derived from 2026 wave 1 actuals. Sum=100.

estimated_dm_timing_prior_years._note: Estimated from historical DM lead spike patterns. First significant DM spike indicates in-home date; subtract 3 days for estimated drop date. All prior years started DM 2-3 weeks earlier than 2026.

weather_context_2026._note: Actual weather conditions (Open-Meteo, 4 priority markets averaged) during key periods.

week_9_cold_snap.conditions: Snow 2/22-2/23 (2-3in), high only 31°F on 2/24, snow again 3/3. Avg high 38.5°F across 9 days.
week_9_cold_snap.impact: Organic leads -69% vs 2025 same week. Combined below-40°F (-46%) and snow (-59%) suppression during Early phase (very high weather sensitivity). Zero DM active (2026 first drop 3/3 vs 2025 first drop 2/15).

wave_1_weather.key_events: Rain on 3/6 (42°F), then spectacular warm stretch 3/8-3/11 (70-81°F sunny), snow squall 3/12 (snowfall 0.35in despite 65°F high), cold snap 3/17-3/18 (38-42°F). Wild weather swings.

wave_2_weather_actual_plus_forecast.days_0_2_actual: 3/20: 64°F light rain, 3/21: 58°F partly cloudy, 3/22: 72°F sunny warm
wave_2_weather_actual_plus_forecast.days_3_8_actual: 3/23: 57°F rain, 3/24: 45°F cool, 3/25: 54°F partly cloudy, 3/26: 67°F sunny, 3/27: 61°F rain, 3/28: 44°F cool

recovery_signal.impact: Week 11 organic (911) was +22% vs 2025 (749). Driven by 3/8-3/11 warm spell (70-81°F). Confirms strong underlying demand when weather cooperates.

warm_streak_multipliers._note: Consecutive warm days (>60°F) amplify organic leads modestly beyond single-day weather multiplier. The sunny_warm weather multiplier (1.35x) already captures most of the warm-day effect; this adds a small compounding bonus for sustained warmth. Cross-validated against all years 2021-2025: 2026 actuals run ~1.08x growth-adjusted comps (excluding outliers), so streak bonuses must be conservative to avoid over-prediction.

temperature_impact._note: Lead volume relative to baseline by temperature range. Validated by 2026 data.

year_over_year_growth._note_growth: +10% YoY growth target is baked into all baselines. No separate growth adjustment needed.
